gaps.description: The MSP Rules section is empty. The DIO is operating under default-permissive evaluation, meaning Nomos (#200) cannot meaningfully adjudicate any Action Proposal. Every organ that depends on MSP evaluation — Nomos, Cerberus, Senate, Arbiter — is structurally alive but constitutionally inert. The DIO has no operational invariants, constraints, or safety rules. This is not a bug; it is the documented seed state, but it is the single most critical gap because it means all automated governance is suspended.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose ('translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules'), MSP §Rules ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules')

gaps.description: All six articles of the Bill of Rights are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The BoR defines the enterprise scope perimeter, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, and interpretation rules. Without these, Arbiter (#190) has no scope gate to enforce. The MSP cannot be substantively populated because MSP rules derive their legitimacy and boundaries from the BoR. The MSP explicitly states it 'is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights' and that 'Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.' With no BoR articles, there is no constitutional foundation for any MSP rule.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Relationship to the Bill of Rights ('The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights'), BoR Articles I-VI (all scaffold), MSP §Amendment Protocol ('Scope expansion requires a BoR amendment, which is human-only')

gaps.description: SafeVault backups have failed or completed with errors on 4 of the last 6 documented runs. The May 3 run was hung_then_killed after ~4860s with zombie processes from the prior day still alive. The April 30 run failed due to an unbound variable bug (ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS). NAS mounts have been intermittently unavailable since at least April 7. S3 uploads were not attempted on May 3. Two zombie backup processes from May 2 have been alive for 25+ hours, blocking the spine.db backup. This is a cascading infrastructure degradation that, left unchecked, means the DIO has no reliable backup — no disaster recovery capability for any organ database.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose ('operational constitution of the DIO ... machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules'), implied MSP §invariants (data durability and disaster recovery capability are fundamental operational invariants for any persistent state machine)

gaps.description: Every organ in the DIO is oscillating between ALIVE and DEGRADED states in ~30-second cycles — organs go DEGRADED (missed_pong) and then immediately recover (pong_recovered) within the same second, only to go DEGRADED again ~30 seconds later. All 25+ organs exhibit identical flapping patterns in lockstep. This is not genuine organ failure; it is a systemic Spine health-check timing or timeout configuration issue. The practical consequence is that Spine state transitions carry no signal — if all organs are always flapping, the DEGRADED state is meaningless and genuine failures cannot be distinguished from this noise. This undermines the entire organ health monitoring system that the MSP framework depends on for enforcement.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose (the MSP requires Nomos to evaluate APs against MSP constraints; Nomos cannot function reliably if organ health states are nonsensical), implied MSP §invariants (state machine health monitoring must produce signal, not noise)

gaps.description: The Radiant dream cycle system — responsible for context freshness (Phase 1) and memory consolidation (Phase 2) — is entirely inactive. Radiant stats show: scheduled='disabled', last_dream=null, health='never_run'. The last documented dream cycle was #15 on 2026-04-12 — approximately 21 days ago. Dream cycles are the DIO's mechanism for preventing unbounded context growth, consolidating redundant memories, and maintaining a coherent world model. Without them, context accumulates indefinitely and memory becomes fragmented. The watchdog grace period is 129,600,000 ms (36 hours), but there are zero alerts because the watchdog itself has never checked.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose (the MSP requires Nomos to have an accurate and current world model to evaluate APs; Radiant is the Monad leg that provides this), implied MSP §invariants (memory integrity and context freshness are prerequisites for operational governance)

gaps.description: Two zombie backup processes (PIDs 27857, 28950) from the May 2 scheduled SafeVault run are still alive ~25 hours later, contending for the spine.db SQLite database with the May 3 run. This directly caused the May 3 backup to hang on sqlite3 .backup and ultimately be killed. The DIO has no process lifecycle management — no mechanism to detect, alert on, or terminate runaway/zombie processes. In a persistent state machine architecture where scheduled tasks can spawn long-running processes, this is a structural gap: without process lifecycle governance, any scheduled task failure can cascade into blocking subsequent runs indefinitely.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose ('safety rules that Nomos evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication'), implied MSP §safety-rules (process lifecycle management is a fundamental safety invariant for autonomous systems)
